Tests that WebMCP toolInvoked and toolResponded events fire correctly.
Enabling WebMCP Domain
Executing imperative tool...
Invoked {
    frameId : <string>
    input : {"text":"hello"}
    invocationId : <string 0>
    toolName : imperative_tool
}
Responded {
    invocationId : <string 0>
    output : {
        text_was : hello
    }
    status : Success
}
Executing declarative tool...
Invoked {
    frameId : <string>
    input : {"text":"hello"}
    invocationId : <string 1>
    toolName : declarative_tool
}
Responded {
    invocationId : <string 1>
    output : {
        declarative : success
    }
    status : Success
}
Executing failing JS tool...
Invoked {
    frameId : <string>
    input : {"text":"hello"}
    invocationId : <string 2>
    toolName : failing_js_tool
}
Responded {
    errorText :
    invocationId : <string 2>
    status : Error
}
Executing failing ModelContext tool (invalid JSON)...
Invoked {
    frameId : <string>
    input : invalid json
    invocationId : <string 3>
    toolName : imperative_tool
}
Responded {
    errorText : Failed to parse input arguments
    invocationId : <string 3>
    status : Error
}
Executing cancelled tool...
Invoked {
    frameId : <string>
    input : {"text":"hello"}
    invocationId : <string 4>
    toolName : abortable_tool
}
Responded {
    errorText :
    invocationId : <string 4>
    status : Canceled
}
